title: chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis
intro: Chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis, commonly known as the Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The Desert Marigold is known for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which bloom in the late winter and early spring. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and acting as an expectorant. The bioactive constituents of the Desert Marigold include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions of treating respiratory issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
benefits: Chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis has many benefits, such as being a rich source of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative properties. These properties can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, while also promoting healthy digestion, immune function, and skin health. Additionally, the plant's bioactive compounds have been found to have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to prevent or alleviate conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease, ultimately improving cognitive function and overall quality of life. By incorporating Chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis into one's diet or using its extracts topically, individuals can experience a range of benefits that can enhance their physical and mental well-being, leading to a more vibrant and fulfilling life.
side_effects: Chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues in some individuals. Consuming large quantities of the plant's extracts or using them for extended periods may lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Additionally, improper handling or preparation of the plant can cause respiratory problems, such as bronchitis or asthma-like symptoms, in people with pre-existing conditions. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional or an experienced herbalist before using Chylismia walkeri subsp. tortilis for medicinal purposes.
